title: Piquant Spicy Indian Rice Dosa
description: A quick-fix South Indian dish featuring a spicy rice and lentil crepe, served in a lacquerware bowl. This sugar-free-friendly recipe combines Indian and Cajun flavors.
instructions: Grind the soaked idli rice and split black lentils into a smooth batter using a blender or food processor.
Heat the ghee or oil in a small pan over medium heat and saute the chopped onion and green chili until the onion is translucent.
Add the cajun spice blend and salt to the pan and stir for 1 minute.
Combine the sauteed mixture with the ground batter and mix well.
Add water to the batter to achieve a thick but pourable consistency.
Heat a non-stick skillet or dosa pan over medium heat and pour a small amount of the batter onto the pan.
Spread the batter evenly to form a thin crepe and cook for 2 minutes or until the bottom is golden brown.
Flip the dosa over and cook for another minute or until the other side is also golden brown.
Serve the piquant spicy Indian rice dosa hot in a lacquerware bowl.
